data.title: Nasdaq Proposes Rule Change for Tokenized Securities Trading
data.description:
Nasdaq has submitted a proposal to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) seeking approval for trading and settlement of tokenized versions of Nasdaq-listed equities and exchange-traded products (ETPs). According to NS3.AI, these tokenized securities would retain the same rights and CUSIP as traditional shares and would be traded on the same order book. The settlement process could optionally utilize blockchain technology through the DTC's distributed ledger platform.

The proposal includes measures to prevent liquidity fragmentation and aims to achieve near-instant settlement, known as T+0. If the SEC approves the rule change, Nasdaq anticipates a potential launch of this initiative in late Q3 2026.

data.title: Analysis: Trump posted an average of 18 tweets per day during his first year in office, mentioning "great" 1400 times.
data.description: A Big Data Analysis of Trump's First Year in Office: 6200 Posts Revealing Crazy Details and Interesting Statistics: 1. "Finger-Tip Maniac": Statistics show that Trump posted a total of 6168 posts in 2025, averaging about 18 posts per day. During his first year in office, from January 20, 2025 to January 20, 2026, the total number of posts was around 6200. 2. Top 5 Most Frequently Used Words: No.1 "Great": Appeared over 1400 times, an absolute catchphrase. No.2 "Never": Appeared 561 times, often used to express "never surrender." No.3 "Border": Appeared 512 times, a core policy focus. No.4 "Biden": Appeared 277 times, still a major target of comparison despite leaving office. No. 5 "Siege": Appeared 276 times, revealing the "wartime president's" mentality. 3. "Golden Posting Time": The most active time of day was 6:00 PM (494 posts throughout the year), and the quietest time was 4:00 AM. 4. Single-Day "Burst" Record: December 1, 2025 set a single-day record with 168 posts. Among them, 147 posts were posted in the two hours between 10:00 PM and midnight, averaging one post every 40 seconds. The most market-destructive "finger": No. 1 US Stock Market "Black Two Days": On April 2, 2025, a sudden post announced tariffs for "Liberation Day." The S&P 500 index plummeted 10.5% in the following 48 hours, wiping out trillions of dollars in market value, marking the worst performance since 2020. No. 2 The Fed's "verbal attacks" fail: In the second half of 2025, the Fed repeatedly posted online criticizing Powell as "too late" and demanding a rate cut to 1%. This triggered extreme market panic, with the 10-year Treasury yield rising instead of falling, breaking through the 4.26% high at the beginning of this year. No. 3 Defense stock "buyback ban": On January 7, 2026, the Fed specifically criticized defense contractors such as Raytheon, threatening to prohibit their stock buybacks and dividends. Lockheed Martin's stock price plummeted 5%. 4. Credit card "interest rate cap": On January 9, 2026, the Fed proposed to forcibly cap credit card interest rates at 10%. Capital One and Synchronous Financial's stock prices both fell by more than 6% that day. 5. Greenland "land purchase" controversy: The Fed again proposed purchasing Greenland and threatened to impose tariffs on opposing European countries. This geopolitical friction shook market confidence in US assets, indirectly contributing to the rise in US Treasury yields. (Jinshi)

data.title: Strategy's Bitcoin holdings currently show a floating profit of $10.813 billion, while Bitmine's Ethereum holdings currently show a floating loss of $3.232 billion.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, Bitcoin treasury company Strategy (MSTR) increased its holdings by 22,305 BTC ($2.125 billion) last week at a price of approximately $95,284. They now hold a total of 709,715 BTC ($64.736 billion), with an average cost of $75,979, resulting in a paper profit of $10.813 billion. Ethereum treasury company Bitmine (BMNR) increased its holdings by 35,268 ETH ($113 million) last week at a price of approximately $3,225. They now hold a total of 4,203,036 ETH ($12.979 billion), with an average cost of $3,857, resulting in a paper loss of $3.232 billion.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Aims to Reduce Deficit Before Trump's Term Ends
data.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Besent has announced efforts to reduce the deficit to 3% of GDP before the end of U.S. President Donald Trump's term. According to Odaily, this goal is part of a broader strategy to stabilize the nation's economy. The focus is on implementing fiscal policies that will support sustainable economic growth while managing the national debt effectively.

data.title: BitMine Immersion Technologies Reveals Cryptocurrency Holdings
data.description: BitMine Immersion Technologies has disclosed its current cryptocurrency holdings, which include 4,203,036 ETH and 192 BTC. According to Odaily, the company also holds Eightco Holdings equity valued at $22 million and $979 million in unencumbered cash. Last week, BitMine increased its Ethereum holdings by 35,268 ETH.

data.title: Last week, global listed companies were net buyers of BTC, totaling $2.15 billion. Strategy invested $2.125 billion to purchase 22,305 Bitcoins.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, as of January 19, 2025 (Eastern Time), global listed companies (excluding mining companies) saw a net purchase of $2.15 billion in Bitcoin last week. Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) announced on January 15th that it invested $2.125 billion to acquire 22,305 Bitcoins at a price of $95,284, bringing its total holdings to 709,715. Japanese listed company Metaplanet did not purchase any Bitcoin last week. In addition, two other companies purchased Bitcoin last week. Daily food content creation company DayDayCook announced on January 15th that it invested $17.8 million to acquire 200 Bitcoins at a price of $88,998, bringing its total holdings to 1,383. On January 16th, the UK-based Bitcoin reserve company BHODL announced an investment of $100,000, acquiring one Bitcoin at a price of $97,800, bringing its total holdings to 159,295 Bitcoins. As of press time, the total number of Bitcoins held by publicly listed companies worldwide (excluding mining companies) is 959,810, with a current market value of approximately $87.32 billion, representing 4.8% of Bitcoin's circulating market capitalization.

data.title: Trump Media Technology Group: Registration for the digital token program begins February 2nd.
data.description: Trump Media Technology Group has announced that the registration date for its recently announced digital token program is February 2, 2026. Following the registration date, Trump Media will partner with Crypto.com to mint digital tokens, list them on the blockchain, and hold these digital assets in custody before distribution. Further details regarding the specific process for allocating and distributing the tokens to eligible shareholders on February 2 will be released later. (Globenewswire)

data.title: US Treasury Secretary Bessant: Trump's deficit reduction target before the end of his term is 3% of GDP.
data.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessant stated that progress is being made towards the goal of reducing the deficit to 3% of GDP before the end of President Trump's term. (Jinshi)

data.title: Satya Nadella says AI race will be won by countries that secure cheap, reliable energy first
data.description:
Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella said the global AI race will not be decided by software alone. He said energy costs will decide which countries pull ahead and which ones fall behind. Satya spoke at the World Economic Forum on Tuesday as governments rush to build AI infrastructure to chase productivity gains and economic growth. He […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/satya-nadella-says-who-wins-ai-race/

data.title: ETHGas Foundation: GWEI token airdrop pricing website is now live.
data.description: The ETHGas Foundation announced on the X platform that the GWEI token airdrop eligibility website is now live, and reward claims will begin at 21:00 Beijing time on January 21st. Eligibility is primarily determined by two criteria: the user's historical Ethereum mainnet gas consumption recorded when generating their Gas ID before the snapshot, serving as "proof of use"; and verified social participation in ETHGas and the "Gas-Free Future Community Initiative".

data.title: Backpack Wallet launches Solana zero-fee trading service
data.description: Backpack Wallet has announced a zero-fee trading experience on the Solana network, with no fees charged for token swaps and cross-chain bridging. This offer is currently only available to Backpack Wallet users.

data.title: Trend Research withdrew another 7,960 ETH from Binance, worth $24.74 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, Trend Research just withdrew another 7,960 ETH ($24.74 million) from Binance, bringing their total purchases for today to 17,899 ETH ($55.58 million). They now hold a total of 644,600 ETH ($2 billion), with an average cost of $3,182.

data.title: The former FTX EU team launched Perpetuals, an AI-powered crypto derivatives platform, which is scheduled to list on Nasdaq this week.
data.description: A team led by Patrick Gruhn and Robin Matzke, former co-founders of FTX Europe (FTX EU), announced the launch of Perpetuals.com (PDC), an AI-powered crypto derivatives platform whose shares will begin trading on Nasdaq this week. Officially launched after Earlyworks Co. acquired Perpetual Markets Ltd., the platform features regulated 24/7 self-clearing trading and blockchain settlement technology. Its goal is to help product issuers create innovative alternatives to the heavily regulated and often predatory Contracts for Difference (CFDs) and perpetual futures markets. (CoinDesk)

data.title: South Korea Uncovers $102 Million Crypto Money Laundering Network
data.description: South Korean customs officials have uncovered a $102 million cryptocurrency money laundering network linked to plastic surgery clinics catering to Chinese tourists. According to NS3.AI, the criminals involved allegedly converted payments from Chinese electronic payment platforms into cryptocurrencies across various jurisdictions before cashing out in South Korea. Authorities have charged three individuals in connection with the scheme and are planning to implement stricter inspections to combat crypto-enabled illicit activities within the expanding medical tourism sector.

data.title: Bridgewater Associates founder Ray Dalio: The monetary order is collapsing.
data.description: Bridgewater Associates founder Ray Dalio stated that the monetary order is collapsing, with fiat currencies and debt no longer held by central banks in the same way. (Jinshi)
